page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-8-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core. This hardware configuration establishes a balanced resource profile suitable for standard computing tasks.

The instance allocates dedicated CPU resources to ensure consistent processing capabilities without the performance fluctuations associated with shared-core environments. No GPU hardware is included in this configuration, and there are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses provided.

This server type is designed for workloads such as general-purpose web serving, application hosting, microservices, and moderate database operations. The balanced ratio of memory to dedicated vCPUs supports stable execution of multi-threaded applications that do not require specialized hardware acceleration or massive memory footprints.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-8-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server within the c4d family, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a balanced resource allocation of 3.88 GB of memory per core, ensuring consistent performance for non-burstable workloads. The instance does not include GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Lacking specialized hardware or extreme memory-to-CPU ratios, this server is positioned as a general-purpose option. It is suitable for standard workloads such as web serving, application hosting, microservices, and distributed development environments where dedicated CPU execution is required without the overhead of specialized compute or storage arrays. This hardware profile offers a reliable foundation for multi-threaded applications requiring stable resource availability.
